exploration_log.aha_moment: The early return at the else branch (line 684) short-circuits the prevArg check; inserting prevArg inspection before that return fixes the case where a dash-prefixed flag value is mistaken for a new flag name.
exploration_log.hindsight: The bug is straightforward once you trace the execution path for --flag -value; the early return is the culprit.

alternatives: Fix in bash V2 script template

solve_notes: In `checkIfFlagCompletion`, when `lastArg` starts with `-` and has no `=`, the code previously returned early before checking whether `prevArg` is a flag expecting a value. Added a check before the early return: if `prevArg` is a flag-argument with a non-boolean flag, return that flag for value completion, suppressing flag-name suggestions for the dash-prefixed value.
task_text:
Fix bash completion v2 not handling flag values that start with a dash

When a flag value legitimately starts with `-` (e.g., `--offset -10`),
the v2 bash completion script interprets it as another flag and offers
flag completions instead of leaving it as a value. Fix the bash
completion logic in `completions.go` to detect when a flag is expecting
a value and suppress flag-name completions.
